title: The Peril of Cymbaline Isle - Session 1 (Solo RPG using Scarlet Heroes, White Box, and more)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IE6oyFF67A0

Daniel (@dungeondive) admits up front that two earlier takes of this session got scrapped — the open-ended start was too intimidating — and instead drops Harlan Peters straight into the city of Hoffen with established kit and a base of operations. The session is openly framed as warts-and-all learning, with credit to channels like Stone Axe Tabletop and Solo RPG Guy that helped shape the approach.

Harlan finishes the build (AC 5, HP 5, Fray d6, short sword for Dex-keyed attack and damage) and rolls his archetype trait Adventuring Archaeologist to 3, with Natural Navigator and Sly Saboteur at 2 and a wildcard point in Street Magician. Base of operations becomes the Crooked Violin, named via Madeline Hale's Table Fables — modestly priced, never quite empty, run by Xavier Swifteye, an aging anti-religious tavern keeper afraid of sleeping alone (which conveniently explains Harlan's lodging).

Adventure setup leans on Scarlet Heroes' Adventure Tags: a Plundered Tribute hook, reframed as a cult bleeding tithes from a local church. Harbour-district official Yazid Nakit fronts the visible antagonist; the deeper shape of the cult is left for investigation scenes. The session demonstrates the urban victory-point loop that drives Scarlet Heroes' solo mode — clues, scenes, and heat tracked alongside threat level.

----

Is Scarlet Heroes' victory-point urban-adventure layer the right scaffolding for solo play, or does its game-loop structure crowd out the looser improvisation that drew you to the genre?
